description: SGLang serving Qwen3.6-27B on AMD R9700 (gfx1201) — DeltaNet-hybrid attention+mamba

panels.description: Requests currently executing in the SGLang scheduler.
panels.title: Running Reqs

panels.description: Requests waiting in the scheduler queue.
panels.title: Queue Reqs

panels.description: Live generation throughput reported by SGLang (tok/s).
panels.title: Gen Throughput (tok/s)

panels.description: Prefix cache hit rate (0-100%). High rate = repeated system prompts being served from cache, reducing TTFT.
panels.title: Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: SGLang GPU utilization % as reported by the engine.
panels.title: GPU Utilization

panels.description: 1 = batch executing via CUDA/HIP graph (faster path), 0 = eager fallback (dynamic shapes).
panels.title: CUDA/HIP Graph

panels.description: Running vs queued requests over time. Queue > 0 for sustained periods = throughput saturation.
panels.title: Scheduler State

panels.description: GPU utilization and forward-pass occupancy. Low occupancy with high utilization = small batches / decode-dominated.
panels.title: GPU Utilization & Forward Occupancy

panels.description: Prefill vs decode token rates derived from counters. When prefill spikes and decode drops, a large prefill is stalling decode batches.
panels.title: Token Throughput (tok/s)

panels.description: Realtime token breakdown by mode: prefill_compute (cold prefill), prefill_cache (hit from prefix cache), decode. A high prefill_cache fraction = strong cache reuse.
panels.title: Tokens by Mode (stacked)

panels.description: Request arrival rate derived from the total request counter.
panels.title: Request Rate (req/s)

panels.description: Prompt token length distribution (p50/p90/p99). Large p99 = occasional very long prompts that dominate prefill time.
panels.title: Prompt Length Distribution (tokens)

panels.description: Time to first token (TTFT) — how long from request arrival to first generated token. Dominated by prefill time. DeltaNet hybrid: mamba-style SSM state init adds to TTFT.
panels.title: Time to First Token (p50/p90/p99)

panels.description: End-to-end request latency (queue + prefill + decode). The user-visible wall-clock latency for streaming-complete responses.
panels.title: E2E Request Latency (p50/p90/p99)

panels.description: Time requests spend waiting in the scheduler queue before prefill begins. Sustained high queue time = engine saturated.
panels.title: Queue Wait Time (p50/p90/p99)

panels.description: Per-stage breakdown of request latency (prefill, decode, etc). Identifies which pipeline stage dominates.
panels.title: Per-Stage Latency (p50/p90)

options.content: **Qwen3.6-27B uses DeltaNet hybrid attention.** `mamba_usage` / mamba slot counts reflect SSM recurrent-state slots, which are finite and cap concurrency independently of KV pool size. When mamba slots exhaust, new requests stall even if KV pool has headroom.

panels.description: KV attention pool, full-attention pool, and mamba SSM state pool utilization (0-100%). Mamba slots cap concurrency for DeltaNet layers.
panels.title: Pool Utilization (KV / Full-Attn / Mamba)

panels.description: KV pool slot counts: used, available (free), and evictable (can be freed by evicting cached prefixes). All in tokens.
panels.title: KV Pool Tokens (used / available / evictable)

panels.description: Mamba SSM state slots: used vs available. When mamba_used approaches max, new requests stall even if KV headroom remains — this is the DeltaNet concurrency ceiling.
panels.title: Mamba SSM Slots (concurrency gate)

panels.description: Pool configuration: max tokens, currently used, configured context length, and paging parameters.
panels.title: Pool Config (max / used / ctx / pages)

panels.description: Retracted requests (aborted mid-generation) and paused requests (temporarily suspended). Elevated values signal backpressure.
panels.title: Retracted & Paused Requests

panels.description: Grammar/tool-calling queue depth — requests held waiting for the FSM/structured-output engine. High = tool-calling saturation.
panels.title: Grammar Queue (tool-calling backpressure)

panels.description: Scheduler new-token ratio: fraction of batch tokens that are new decode tokens vs prefill tokens. Near 1.0 = pure decode batch. Near 0 = prefill-heavy.
panels.title: New Token Ratio (decode vs prefill mix)

panels.description: Forward-pass batch occupancy %. Low occupancy means small batches (decode-dominated or idle). High occupancy = well-packed batches.
panels.title: Forward-Pass Occupancy %

panels.description: Prefix cache hit rate over time. Sustained values above 70% indicate good reuse of system prompts or long shared contexts. Drops indicate new/diverse prompt patterns.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate Over Time

panels.description: Breakdown of token processing: cold prefill (compute), cache-hit prefill (served from prefix cache), and decode. A wide prefill_cache band = high cache utility.
panels.title: Cache Hits vs Cold Prefill vs Decode (stacked)

panels.description: GPU utilization percent on the R9700 inference node (from node_exporter DRM metrics).
panels.title: GPU Utilization (HW)

panels.description: VRAM used vs total. R9700 has 32 GiB; at --mem-fraction 0.875 SGLang reserves ~28 GiB (weights + KV pool + mamba state).
panels.title: VRAM

panels.description: GPU temperature, Celsius. R9700 throttles at ~110°C junction.
panels.title: GPU Temperature

panels.description: Available GPU memory at startup (GB). Constant; useful to verify SGLang sees the full 32 GiB.
panels.title: Startup GPU Memory (GB)

panels.description: Total engine startup time (seconds). Compare across restarts to detect regressions.
panels.title: Engine Startup Time

panels.description: Time taken to load model weights from disk into VRAM.
panels.title: Weight Load Time

panels.description: Startup GPU memory available at engine init. Should reflect the full 32 GiB of R9700 VRAM.
panels.title: Startup VRAM Available (GB)

options.content: Spec-decode is **not enabled** for this SGLang deployment. `sglang:spec_accept_length` and `sglang:spec_accept_rate` will be zero/absent.

panels.description: Speculative decode acceptance length. Will be zero — spec-decode is disabled on this deployment.
panels.title: Speculative Decode (disabled — should be flat 0)

title: SGLang
